description:
Two layers of integration test:

1. Mockito-driven end-to-end: a scratch bl repo, balls-plugin-github
   and balls-plugin-github-issues both registered, mockito serving as
   GH API. Drive a full lifecycle:
   - bl create task → push half opens GH issue (mock POST)
   - GH-side: simulate someone closing the issue → next bl sync
     pulls it → balls task closes (authoritative close-mirror).
   - Simulate a new GH issue → next bl sync auto-creates a balls
     task → next push tags it with [bl-xxxx] on GH.
   - Drive the forge plugin in parallel (deferred-mode review opens
     a PR via mock, merge → gate child closes).
   The harness lives in tests/ and is part of `make check`.

2. Live sandbox plan: a documented test script that runs the same
   sequence against a real GH repo (operator provides PAT and a
   sandbox repo name). NOT part of `make check`; gated behind an
   env var (BALLS_PLUGIN_LIVE_TEST=1) so coverage gate and CI are
   not network-dependent. This is what we run at Epic-B close time
   against a fresh dummy repo to confirm real GitHub connectivity.

WHY two layers: 100% coverage in `make check` cannot depend on the
real GH API (flaky, rate-limited, requires secrets in CI). Mockito
proves the wire-protocol contract; live test proves the real-API
contract. Both matter; separating them is what lets the gate stay
strict.

ACCEPTANCE:
- tests/integration_mockito.rs drives the full lifecycle scenario;
  failure modes (rate limit, conflict, ingest cap) covered.
- tests/live_sandbox.sh is a runnable script (no tests under
  cfg(test)) with documented env vars; reads PAT from $GITHUB_PAT;
  prints PASS/FAIL per step; safe to re-run (idempotent on the
  sandbox repo state).
- The mockito test counts toward the 100% coverage gate; the live
  test does not (it's behind cfg(feature = "live_test") or env-gated).
- 100% coverage; under 300 lines per file.
title: Integration test harness: mockito end-to-end + live sandbox plan
